purpose: Canonicalize URLs by normalizing scheme, host, port, path, and query ordering for cache keys, comparison, and request signing.

endpoints.description: Canonicalize an absolute URL without making a network request.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Deterministic URL normalization for web workflows.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when comparing URLs, generating cache keys, preparing signatures, or deduplicating request descriptors.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use to validate ownership, crawl pages, or resolve redirects over the network.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Submit absolute URL and normalization options. 2) Receive canonical_url. 3) Use canonical_url as a stable comparison key.

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use the WHATWG URL class. Remove default ports for http:80 and https:443. Sort query parameters by key and value only when sort_query is true. Preserve duplicate query keys.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: URLSearchParams sorting can change duplicate ordering. Percent encoding normalization must be deterministic but should not double-decode reserved characters.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Build canonicalization as a pure function returning canonical_url and changes for transparent tests.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not call fetch, do not resolve redirects, and do not infer HTTPS availability.

test_cases.name: lowercase host and drop default port
